steps.title: Identify the angle structure from the figure
steps.content: Line $l$ is the transversal. From the figure, angle $w$ at line $j$ is in the same relative position as the marked $54^\circ$ angle at line $k$.

steps.title: Use the converse of corresponding angles
steps.content: If two lines cut by a transversal have congruent corresponding angles, then the two lines are parallel. So if $w=54$, angle $w$ matches the corresponding $54^\circ$ angle at line $k$, which proves $j \parallel k$.

steps.title: Eliminate information from only one intersection
steps.content: The choices involving $y$ or $z$ describe angles at line $k$ only, so they do not compare line $k$ to line $j$. The choice $x=126$ does not make the corresponding angles equal. Therefore, the sufficient additional information is $w=54$, and choice A is correct.
